hypothesis: This experiment tests whether a LOSS AVERSION frame ('you're losing sales right now') converts better than the current CONVENIENCE frame ('order anytime') because loss aversion produces ~2x the psychological response of equivalent gains (Kahneman), and vendors viscerally feel the pain of missed sales on non-market days. The entire page narrative shifts from 'here's a nice option' to 'you're actively bleeding revenue — stop it in 15 minutes.'

swaps.baseline: Let Your Customers Order From You Anytime
Set up a simple online storefront in minutes. Your customers browse your products, place orders, and pick up on your schedule.
swaps.challenger: Every day without online ordering, your regulars can't buy from you. Get a simple storefront live in 15 minutes.

swaps.baseline: Your customers want to buy from you between markets. Now they can.
swaps.challenger: Every week without a storefront, you're leaving money on the table.
